nodes.title: Validate inputs before the loop

anchor.snippet: if (typeof callbackFn !== 'function') throw new TypeError(String(callbackFn) + ' is not a function');

nodes.why: Early checks give clear errors and stop invalid calls before iteration starts.
nodes.alternative: Skip validation and let the loop fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: More predictable behavior with tiny upfront checks.

nodes.title: Snapshot length and skip holes

anchor.snippet: const len = arr.length >>> 0; // snapshot length once

nodes.why: Snapshotting matches native behavior when the array changes, and the own-property check avoids calling the predicate for missing slots.
nodes.alternative: Loop with `i < arr.length` and process every index.
nodes.tradeoff: Closer to native semantics, with a bit more loop logic.

nodes.title: Keep values only when predicate is truthy
nodes.why: This is the core filter rule: evaluate each present value and push only the ones that pass.
nodes.alternative: Map values first, then remove non-matches in a second pass.
nodes.tradeoff: Single-pass and easy to read, while still supporting `thisArg`.
